habitat: African savannah habitats; deserts; woodlands; river flood-plains; and open grasslands
story: Discover the stunning Northern Carmine Bee Eater (*Merops nubicus*), a member of the diverse Meropidae family. Native to African savannah habitats; deserts; woodlands; river flood-plains; and open grasslands, this bird faces various environmental challenges. Currently classified as Least Concern (LC) on the IUCN Red List, the Northern Carmine Bee Eater represents the delicate balance of its native region. Understanding its habitat requirements and distribution is key to effective conservation planning. This species contributes to the richness of avian diversity, and this poster offers an artistic glimpse into its world.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
